# terrible_check_point_mgmt_cp_mgmt_threat_exception_facts (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `dereference_group_members` (Boolean) Indicates whether to dereference "members" field by details level for every object in reply. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `details_level` (String) The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `exception_group_name` (String) The name of the exception-group.
- `exception_group_uid` (String) The UID of the exception-group.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filter` (String) Search expression to filter the rulebase. The provided text should be exactly the same as it would be given in Smart Console. The logical operators in the expression ('AND', 'OR') should be provided in capital letters. If an operator is not used, the default OR operator applies. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `filter_settings` (String) Sets filter preferences.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `layer` (String) Layer that the rule belongs to identified by the name or UID.
- `limit` (Number) No more than that many results will be returned. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `name` (String) The name of the layer containing the parent threat rule. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `offset` (Number) Skip that many results before beginning to return them. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `order` (String) Sorts results by the given field. By default the results are sorted in the ascending order by name. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `package` (String) Name of the package.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `rule_name` (String) The name of the parent rule.
- `show_membership` (Boolean) Indicates whether to calculate and show "groups" field for every object in reply. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_object_dictionary` (Boolean) N/A

### Read-Only

- `ansible_facts` (String) The checkpoint object facts.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
